# What Is an Agentic System?

## Learning Objective

Define an agentic system in operational terms and separate the model call from the production boundary around it.

## Core Concept

An agentic system is not production-shaped because it calls tools. It becomes production-shaped when the system has explicit tool policy, state boundaries, traces, evals, context control, rollout criteria, and rollback discipline.

For this lab, use the following distinction:

- A workflow has a known control path. It may call models, but orchestration is fixed.
- An agent chooses part of the path at runtime. Tool selection, intermediate observations, and stopping criteria become part of the system behavior.

The interesting engineering work sits around the model: what the runtime is allowed to read, what it records, how it is evaluated, and how failure is bounded.

## Runnable Command

```bash
python scripts/run_all_examples.py --example workflow_baseline
```

## Expected Output

The command lists files from `data/toy_repos/buggy_calc` and reports deterministic matches for division-related text. No model is required.

## Reflection Questions

- Which parts of your current agent designs are actually workflows?
- What state is authoritative: prompt text, tool output, trace, or durable storage?
- Which tool calls would require human approval in your production environment?

## References

See @anthropic_effective_agents and @openai_agents_docs.
